formative.title: Nature Walk Observation Checklist
formative.description: Teacher-led outdoor walk where students identify living things in the environment

items.criterion: Explains why something is living (moves, grows, needs food)
Names at least 8 different living things

formative.method: Teacher observation with checklist, verbal questioning

formative.title: Living vs Non-Living Sorting Activity
formative.description: Students sort picture cards into 'living' and 'non-living' categories on paper plates

items.criterion: Correctly sorts at least 8 living things
Correctly identifies at least 6 non-living items
Explains why each item belongs in its category

formative.title: Plant Needs Labeling Check
formative.description: Students label a plant diagram and answer 'what if' prediction questions

items.criterion: Predicts what happens if plant has no water
Predicts what happens if plant has no sunlight

formative.method: Worksheet with diagram and short answers

formative.title: Animal Movement Matching Game
formative.description: Students match animals to how they move (walk, fly, swim, crawl)

items.criterion: Correctly matches 8+ animals to movement type

formative.method: Visual matching activity with teacher observation

summative.title: Living Things Unit Test
summative.description: End-of-unit assessment covering all learning objectives

sections.name: Part A: Living or Not Living
sections.description: Look at the picture and circle whether it is living or not living

sections.name: Part B: Label the Plant
sections.description: Label the parts of the plant (roots, stem, leaves, flower, soil)

sections.name: Part C: Animal Movement
sections.description: Draw a line to match each animal with how it moves

sections.name: Part D: What Do Plants Need?
sections.description: Choose the correct answer for what plants need to survive

sections.name: Part E: Explain Your Thinking
sections.description: Draw and write about a living thing you saw today and why it is living

quiz_questions.question: Which of these is a living thing?
A toy car is living because it can move.
quiz_questions.explanation: Living things move on their own and need food and water. A toy cannot move by itself.

quiz_questions.question: What do plants need to grow?
Match each animal to how it moves:
Name three things that are living.
What happens to a plant if you give it no water?
quiz_questions.explanation: Plants need water to stay alive and make food. Without water, they cannot survive.

quiz_questions.question: Is a flower living? Why or why not?
quiz_questions.correct_answer: Yes, because it grows, needs water and sunlight, and responds to the environment

quiz_questions.question: Put these in the right group: Sun, Cat, Book, Tree, Car, Butterfly
What is the job of the roots?
options: To hold the plant and take in water
quiz_questions.correct_answer: To hold the plant and take in water
